name: Agent Framework Bloat
summary: Stacking heavy abstractions above the model call — chains, runnables, callbacks, agents, executors — that obscure the underlying tool-call loop. smolagents argues for staying ~1k LoC and minimal abstractions above raw code.

raised_by.note: Stated design value in smolagents docs: 'simplicity' as an explicit anti-bloat stance.

evidence.title: smolagents index — Simplicity
evidence.quote: Simplicity: The logic for agents fits in ~thousand lines of code. We kept abstractions to their minimal shape above raw code!

rejected_reason: Framework design stance rather than a recurring problem with cross-evidence. The complaint surfaces only as one framework's marketing differentiator (smolagents); no second independent named occurrence of the same problem with a competing fix. Catalog's bar (recurring problem across products/years) is not met. Reconsider if 2+ independent sources name this anti-pattern with the same shape.
